travel from the foundation level up to their final position.

instead of barely-noticeable 20px nudge.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix word placement to build tower from base

Iterate placement loop bottom→top so base rows (wide, appear first in
animation) win ties over observation deck rows. Big/prominent words now
land at the foundation and visibly appear first as the tower builds up.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Make tower build animation visibly bottom-to-top

Increase row stagger from 40ms to 100ms and reduce per-row duration
from 500ms to 400ms, making the cascading bottom-to-top build effect
clearly perceivable (~1.1s total for 8 rows).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Extend profanity filter with anatomical and explicit terms

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add host_wake_all_slots to pre-warm shadow FX before Song Mode playback

With Link Audio, Move's native track audio routes through shadow slots
for FX processing. When a track goes quiet, the slot idles and skips
rendering. On step transitions, the sudden audio arrival caused glitches
because FX state was stale. Wake all slots at playback start and each
step advance so FX chains run on zeros before audio arrives.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Fix idle agent removal for subagents and page reconnect

  subagent_done handlers

  on page load / reconnect
- Subagents now properly fade out when they complete

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: deletion validation crash + live status/priority sync in task panel

Bug 1 — Deletion silently failing (discussions & comments):
Root cause: Mongoose 8 rejects empty strings for `required: true` String
fields. Both deleteComment and deleteDiscussion do `document.body = ''`
then `document.save()`, which throws a ValidationError (500). The client
catch block then refetches the full list, reverting the optimistic delete
and making it look like nothing happened.
Fix: pass `{ validateBeforeSave: false }` to save() in both controllers —
soft-deleting by clearing the body is intentional, so schema validation
is intentionally bypassed for these delete operations only.

Bug 2 — Live status/priority not updating in side panel:
Root cause: TaskDetailModal keeps a local `fullTask` state that is only
wired up to the `task:updated` socket event. When a task is moved via
Kanban drag-and-drop the server emits `task:moved` (not `task:updated`),
which only updates the Zustand store via useSocketEvents — the modal's
local state never sees it, so the status/priority buttons stay stale.
Fix: add a useEffect that watches `task.status / priority / dueDate /
completedAt` from the Zustand store (always kept current by useSocketEvents
for all event types) and merges changes into fullTask. A storeSyncMountedRef
skips the initial mount so the "Live" badge doesn't flash on open. The
equality check inside setFullTask prevents unnecessary re-renders when
values haven't actually changed.

commit.message:
docs(comments): graph as projection — universal process model, variable boundary grain (pre-ADR-026)

Generalises beyond SDLC: the 4 primitives are universal; the SDLC graph is one instantiation.
Different process flows (SDLC, CI/CD, business approval, data pipelines) are different
boundary drawings over the same event stream — views, not separate systems.
Grain = the chosen boundary for a unit of work in a given automation context.
Automated flows accept fine grain; human-supervised flows need coarse grain.
Spawn is grain-crossing recursion; parentRunId links across grain levels.
Extends PROJECTIONS_AND_INVARIANTS.md profiles concept to cross-domain projection.
Proposes: pre-ADR-026 (variable boundary grain), spec §3 "Grain and Domain Projection",
  process:* facet namespace for domain-agnostic OL events.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: Strava storage_failed — drop fitness_connections device_id FK

The fitness_connections.device_id FK to nodes(device_id) causes all
Strava OAuth callbacks to fail with storage_failed. The connect route
already validates device exists + is active before issuing the OAuth
URL, making the DB-level FK redundant and fragile.

Fixes:
- migration-v8: DROP FK constraint + make device_id nullable
- fitness-data.ts: deviceId || null to handle missing device gracefully
- Add console.error logging to storeConnection for future debugging

commit.message:
docs: add agent design philosophy document (#250)

* docs: add agent design philosophy document

Covers five areas: agent-tool relationship (all tools default available,
@mention as guidance), skill system (three-layer loading, priority
resolution, gating logic), security model (bash three-tier classification,
async permission queue, read-before-write), desktop vs CLI agent
differences (SOUL persistent identity, GUI permission flow), and
evolution directions (tool expansion, skill ecosystem, sub-agent depth).

Add reference in CLAUDE.md supplementary docs table.

Closes #206

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* docs: fix inaccuracies in agent design document per review

- Section 1: clarify that skill, skill_resource, and spawn_agent have
  special registration logic (factory functions, depth guard), not
  unconditional registration
- Section 2 pseudocode: add built-in exception branches for the three
  factory-created tools
- Section 2 table: correct User skill loading method from read_skill
  to discover_external_skills (read_skill is single-skill CRUD, not
  the discovery pipeline)
- Add note distinguishing discovery vs CRUD APIs

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* docs: clarify external skill source priority levels

Only claude source gets priority 1; cursor/agents/opencode fall into
priority 2 (same as built-in). Update table and description to match
sourcePriority() implementation.

fallback responses, ensuring consistent text output.

feat: Phase 5 — Shift Count Tracking & Visibility Rules

Backend:

  confirmed shift count and full shift list for the season
- GET /roster/summary?season_id: batch-optimized dashboard stats —
  active volunteers, open shifts, fill rate %, upcoming shifts (7 days)
- GET /roster/export?season_id: CSV download with name/email/role/count/shift details

Frontend:

  per volunteer, search filter, footer totals, Export CSV button
- CountBadge: color-coded circle (gray=0, amber=1-2, green=3+)

  open shifts, fill rate, upcoming; quick links to Shifts and Roster
- Dashboard (volunteer): "You are scheduled for X shifts this season" count card
  with link to My Schedule; counts only confirmed assignments
- Both dashboards handle no-season state gracefully
- Roster nav link added for managers

Add userInterface/ directory and Composable stub to module code generation

New UserInterfaceStubGenerator produces a write-once @Composable stub file
with Column + Text placeholder and ViewModel import. ModuleSaveService now
creates the userInterface/ subdirectory and generates the stub on first save,
preserving existing UI code on re-save.

Add smart branch resolution: fetch from origin before creating worktrees

When creating a new worktree, now checks if the branch exists on origin
first and creates a tracking worktree if so, instead of always creating
a new user-prefixed branch. Also adds slash-to-dash directory name
sanitization, duplicate checkout detection, and fixes --rm to detect
the actual branch name from the worktree.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add two-VM Docker demo with sandboxed code sync

Implements end-to-end demo of two Maggie VMs in Docker containers sharing
code via content-addressed sync and exchanging messages over HTTP, with
three-layer security (capability manifests, hash verification, process
restriction).

New features:
- HttpClient primitive (get:/post:body:/put:body:/delete:)
- Sandbox class (run:/restrictions/isRehydrated:) for default sandboxed
  execution of rehydrated code
- DiskCache wired into CLI startup, sync pull, and Transfer RPC
- .mag stub files for HttpClient/HttpServer/HttpRequest/HttpResponse

Demo infrastructure:
- Dockerfile (multi-stage, Go 1.24)
- docker-compose.yml with vm-a and vm-b services
- Demo Maggie source (Greeter class, HTTP messaging, sandbox execution)
- Launch script (scripts/demo-two-vm.sh) with --keep and --verbose flags
- Demo README with architecture diagram and security model documentation

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Switch settlement keying from Poi.Name to Poi.SettlementId

Settlements were keyed by flavor name (e.g. "Grassgate") in PlayerState.Settlements,
which is fragile if flavor generation changes. Now uses coordinate-based SettlementId
(e.g. "s16_5") which is stable. Poi.Name is retained only for display purposes.

feat: add client certificate auto-select and custom URL scheme handler

Add two features for enterprise intranet compatibility:

1. Auto-select client certificate when server requests mTLS — picks
   the first certificate from the system keychain so enterprise auth
   (Midway/SSO) works without manual intervention.

2. Delegate custom URL schemes (acme://, etc.) to the OS via
   shell.openExternal() so native enterprise apps can be launched
   from within Tappi.

commit.message:
Split monolith into client/ and server/ for separate deployment

- server/: Express + Drizzle ORM + Supabase PostgreSQL (Railway)
  - Convert SQLite schema to PostgreSQL (pgTable, serial, boolean, timestamp)
  - 16 Next.js API routes → 9 Express router files
  - File uploads via Supabase Storage (multer)
  - PostgreSQL seed script

- client/: Next.js frontend (Vercel)
  - Remove backend dependencies (better-sqlite3, drizzle)
  - All fetch calls use NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL environment variable
  - SWR fetcher prepends API_URL automatically

commit.message:
fix: suppress Claude Code workspace trust prompt on provisioned VMs (#2192)

The "Quick safety check: Is this a project you created or one you trust?"
prompt fires per-workspace and is not suppressed by hasCompletedOnboarding
or --dangerously-skip-permissions (anthropics/claude-code#28506).

Fix: inject a workspace trust entry keyed by $HOME into ~/.claude.json
with hasTrustDialogAccepted: true. The JSON is now constructed on the
remote side so $HOME resolves to the actual path (/root, /home/user, etc).

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: prevent invisible booking sheet from capturing clicks on desktop

The sheet had z-index 1001 with opacity 0 but no pointer-events: none,
so clicks on the trigger were hitting the invisible sheet instead.

accordingly. Unknown URLs require user trust confirmation before
fetching.

commit.message:
Enforce 3% minimum stop-loss floor on Elliott wave signals

Analysis of 127 resolved signals showed Elliott's wave-based SL placement
puts stops too tight (median 1.8% from entry, 52% of losses have SL < 2%).
Widening to a 3% floor recovers 18 of 31 losses, improving WR from 35% to 73%.
